ensembles.name: North Carolina congressional ensemble (ALARM 50-State Simulations)
ensembles.source: ALARM Project, 50-State Redistricting Simulations (Harvard Dataverse, CC0)
ensembles.description: Real NC congressional ensemble (5,000 redist SMC plans) normalized from ALARM's published per-district statistics under the 2020 presidential proxy.
caveats: DRAFT: distributions are computed from ALARM's published statistics, but the payload stays draft until the consistency checks are expert-reviewed. Percentiles are only meaningful relative to the documented constraint set.
The seat and share measures use the 2020 presidential proxy (pre_20), not ALARM's e_dem/pr_dem multi-election composites and not congressional election results.

Compared-plan values (seat counts and district shares) are computed by Honeycombing from VEST 2020 precinct centroid assignment (descriptive_with_assignment_caveat). Calibration against ALARM's exact precinct assignment for the same reference geometry shows a maximum rank-sorted district share difference of 0.12pp.

The ensemble was simulated for the 2020 redistricting cycle with the 2022 ratified congressional map as its reference plan. Percentile positions describe where a plan sits in this simulated distribution; they are not evidence of intent or legal injury.
No H3 cell-level measure yet: cell projection requires ALARM's plan assignment matrices (NC_cd_2020_plans.rds), which this adapter does not ingest.

ensembles.name: North Carolina congressional ensemble summary (MOCK)
ensembles.description: Mocked NC congressional ensemble summary that validates the ensemble explainer schema end-to-end.
caveats: MOCK PAYLOAD: every number below is fabricated to exercise the schema and UI. It is not an ensemble analysis of North Carolina and must never be cited or displayed as evidence.
